sections.text: Fluid pipes use a pressure system to control vertical flow. Fluids move freely horizontally and downward. Pushing fluid upward requires mechanical force from a Pipe Pump.

Each pipe tracks its own pressure in milliBar (mBar). Passive pressure from fluid weight is too weak for upward flow. A Pipe Pump injects active pressure that propagates through connected pipes. Horizontal propagation is free; each block upward costs 100 mBar.
